title: Feature-Request: Tabs: show folder/path for connections with duplicate names
author: justin-oestmann
body_snippet:
Hey

When I open two connections that have the same display name but are stored in different folders, the tab titles are identical. This makes it impossible to tell which tab belongs to which folder.

Example:

Folder A → Linux.11

Folder B → Linux.11

Opened at the same time, both tabs show only Linux.11

Request
Add an option in Settings to include the folder (or full folder path) in the tab title. For example:

Linux.11 — Folder A

Linux.11 — Folder B

or 

Linux.11 / Folder A

Linux.11 / Fol...

iterations.description: Bulk verified: commit bb289379 exists (MEDIUM confidence)

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: its has colour mapping as well but not fully functional but yes yours point is valid

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Triage refresh (P4 version-label debt): this issue is tagged with legacy version labels. Please retest on the latest build (fork v1.79.x or upstream dev), then share reproducible steps and expected vs actual behavior. If still valid, keep open and relabel to current triage scope (for example Need 2 check). If fixed, close with resolution.

Current legacy version labels: 1.78.*

notes:
AI triage (claude): Valid UX enhancement to disambiguate duplicate connection names in tabs by appending folder/path, improving usability for users with many similarly-named connections across different folders.
Approach: When building tab header text, check if multiple open connections share the same display name and append the parent folder path as suffix (e.g. 'Server [Prod/EU]') to disambiguate
